comment: SHA-256 (truncated to 16 hex chars) of each rendered USER prompt template at the SENTINEL_RESUME placeholder + sentinel target context (now including target_jd as of M8.B AND is_internship as of M9.5). Run npm test (lib/llm/__tests__/prompts.test.ts) — it asserts the live hashes equal these. M8.A bumped v2 → v3 (system prompt + reasoning-first JSON + <resume_text> delimiters). M8.B bumped v3 → v4 (target_jd added to fit, top_strengths, missing_signal). M9.5 bumped v4 → v5 (internshipPreamble prepended to all 8 queries when ctx.is_internship=true; sentinel now sets is_internship=true so every per-query hash captures the preamble). Cache namespace bumped to apeds:v5 in lib/llm/perceive.ts in lockstep. Note: SYSTEM_PROMPT changes are NOT captured here — bump cache manually if SYSTEM_PROMPT itself changes. If a template intentionally changes, regenerate via `node --experimental-strip-types -e "import('./lib/llm/prompts.ts').then(m => console.log(JSON.stringify(m.hashPromptTemplates(), null, 2)))"` AND bump the cache version in lib/llm/perceive.ts (apeds:vN → apeds:vN+1) so stale cached completions don't pollute new prompts.
